question.text: What is the maximum number of clients a part-time investment adviser can have at any point of time?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI IA Regulations cap the number of clients for a part-time investment adviser at 75 at any point of time. **The Trap:** 50 is too restrictive and 100 is a commonly assumed round number. The specific regulatory limit is 75. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A covers this in the regulatory framework chapter. The distinction between part-time and full-time IA requirements is a frequently tested area. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A part-time IA must use the term 'part-time investment adviser' in all client correspondence — missing this nomenclature requirement can lead to regulatory action.
